title: Multi-Country Health Dashboard Platform
tagline: Five countries. One source of truth. Zero manual exports.
problem: Programme teams across 5 countries were manually reconciling data from DHIS2, CommCare, and partner spreadsheets — a 3-day process every reporting cycle that routinely produced conflicting numbers and delayed donor submissions.
solution: Designed and built a unified Python ETL pipeline ingesting all source systems into a centralised PostgreSQL warehouse, with automated Power BI dashboards refreshing daily and anomaly alerts firing on threshold breaches.
impact: Eliminated the 3-day reconciliation cycle entirely, cut reporting errors by 94%, and gave country teams live visibility into immunisation coverage for the first time in the programme's history.

title: Automated ETL Pipeline & Reporting System
tagline: From raw API calls to board-ready reports — fully automated.
problem: Eight separate data sources (APIs, flat files, Google Sheets, PostgreSQL) were being joined manually in Excel each week. A single upstream format change could silently corrupt downstream reports for days before anyone noticed.
solution: Built an n8n-orchestrated ETL layer with Python transformation scripts, schema validation on every ingest, and automated delivery of formatted reports to stakeholders on a configurable schedule.
impact: Analysts recovered 12+ hours per week previously lost to data wrangling. Upstream changes are now caught at the pipeline boundary, not in a donor meeting.

title: National HIV/TB Data Quality Framework
tagline: Turning 50+ partner reports into one trustworthy national dataset.
problem: The national HIV/TB programme received monthly reports from 50+ implementing partners using inconsistent formats, missing fields, and duplicate records. Auditors had flagged data quality as a risk to $50M+ in Global Fund grant compliance.
solution: Designed a DQA framework with automated validation rules, a partner-facing submission portal, and compliance monitoring dashboards that flagged delinquent or anomalous submissions in real time.
impact: Brought the programme into full audit compliance within two reporting cycles. Partners now submit to a single standard, and the MOH has a real-time view of national coverage data.

title: Self-Hosted Infrastructure Stack
tagline: Production-grade data services on commodity hardware — no cloud bill.
problem: Client data services were scattered across paid SaaS tools with poor integration, rising costs, and no self-hosted fallback. Sensitive programme data was leaving the country boundary unnecessarily.
solution: Stood up a Proxmox cluster running containerised services — n8n automation, PostgreSQL, Grafana, and a reverse proxy with automated SSL — giving clients full data sovereignty and a reproducible infrastructure blueprint.
impact: Reduced monthly infrastructure spend by 70%, gave clients full control of their data residency, and produced a repeatable stack that has since been deployed for two additional programmes.
